title: Sarah Paine - Why Putin and Xi can't escape geography
summary: Military historian Sarah Paine argues that global power dynamics for two millennia have been shaped by a divide between continental empires seeking security through territorial conquest (China, Russia) and maritime powers seeking prosperity through trade (Britain, US). The framework explains how geography constrains strategic options for leaders like Putin and Xi.
writeup: Military historian Sarah Paine lays out a framework dividing the past two millennia of global power into continental empires that pursue security through territorial conquest (China, Russia) and maritime powers that pursue prosperity through trade (Britain, the United States). The central claim is that geography constrains the strategic options available to leaders, and the conversation applies this lens to Putin and Xi. The takeaway: continental and maritime orientations produce different incentives, with land powers favoring expansion and sea powers favoring commerce.
